questionHtml: Explain the reasons for antibiotics secreted into soil not harming insects or other animals in the soil.
markschemeHtml:
1. antibiotics (only) affect/kill/block processes in bacteria/prokaryotes / do not affect eukaryotes;
  2. metabolism/protein synthesis/ribosomes/cell walls not targeted in insects/animals/eukaryotes;
  3. no cell walls in animals (so antibiotics cannot attack cell walls);
  4. antibiotics are secreted (by microbes/fungi) to prevent competition (with other microbes);

meta.title: Explain the reasons for antibiotics secreted into soil not harming insects or other animals in the soil.
breadcrumbLabels: C3.2.13. Antibiotics as chemicals that block processes occurring in bacteria but not in eukaryotic cells
